summary.pilot_detail: Controls validated. At cos>=0.20 (threshold above random noise floor): knowledge absorption=0.0000, shuffled=0.0000, random=0.0000, first-letter=0.9000. Key finding: first-letter absorption is DRAMATICALLY higher than knowledge absorption. GPT-2 Small SAE features align strongly with first-letter probe directions (max cosine 0.38-0.86) but NOT with knowledge probe directions (max cosine 0.14-0.20). This is consistent with: (a) GPT-2 having dedicated letter-identity features but not geographic-knowledge features in this SAE layer, or (b) knowledge is encoded non-linearly / across multiple features rather than in single absorbing features. Controls establish a false-positive rate of 0% at cos>=0.20, validating the methodology.

interpretation.separation: First-letter significantly exceeds knowledge (p<0.05 at cos>=0.10-0.30)
interpretation.conclusion: Absorption is domain-specific: strong for first-letter syntactic features, absent for geographic knowledge features on GPT-2 Small. This does NOT imply absorption never occurs for knowledge -- it may appear on larger models (Gemma 2B) or models with richer geographic knowledge. The controls are valid: both shuffled and random controls show 0% at cos>=0.20.
